article.title: Uber L4 OA
article.content:
4 questions to solve in 90 min 1-easy , 2-medium, 1-hard

1. [EASY] Given a array of integers return "EVEN" if the sum of integers at even positions is greater then sum of integers at odd position , return "ODD if the sum of integers at odd position is greater then sum of integers at even position else return "EQUAL" if both sums are equal

2. [MEDIUM] Given an integer matrix we have to find the length of the longest diagonal which this pattern : 1, 2, 0, 2, 0, .... i.e. 1 at the 0th index and,  2 and 0 will alternate.
PS: diagonal and anti-diagonal both should be considered

3. [MEDIUM] Given 4 arrays wins, draws, scored, concended of size N these arrays represent the stat of N teams means wins[i] represent the number of wins of i'th team , draws[i] represent the number of draws of i'th team , scored[i] represent the score of i'th team and conceded[i] represent the concede of i'th team (TBH i don't know what this **conceded** means). Now for every win a team is rewarded 3 points and for every draw team is rewarded 1 point, now we want to find the highest and second highest scoring team and if there is a tie between the points it will be resolved by taking the difference between scored[i] - conceded[i].
Return the indexes of the team.
ex: wins : [1, 2, 3]
    draw : [1, 1, 1]
    scored : [10, 20, 30]
    conceded: [12, 23, 12]
   
    points for team 0 = 1*3 + 1*1 = 4
    points for team 1 = 2*3 + 1*1 = 7
    points for team 2 = 3*3 + 1*1 = 10

    return {2,1}
PS: if there is a tie between teams points it will be resolved by taking the difference bewteen scored[i] - conceded[i]
    
    

4. [HARD] Given the rows and cols of a matrix and list representing the positions of those cell whose value is 1 and other cells have value 0, now we want to output a vector of length 5 in which the i'th index represent the number of 2X2 matrices which have i number of 1's
Constraints: 2<= rows <=1E5 
             2<= cols <= 1E5
PS: By checking every 2X2 matrix will not work as it will TLE

post.content: Bro, can I complete all the above questions?\n

For question 3, you can maintain a min-heap of size 2. We need a min-heap so that we can easily remove the lower scoring team to make space for a high scoring team in the min-heap.\nThe min-heap approach would be more space efficient (only two elements are stored in the min-heap) than sorting. This technique will be helpful if there are a lot of teams.\nSimilar to sorting, you will need to implement a custom comparator for the min-heap to handle the scoring and tie-breaking logic as given in the question.

The constraints of the fourth question seem a bit strange. The time complexity for checking every 2x2 matrix isn\'t any different from checking each element of the matrix (i.e., $O(mn)$), and we would need to iterate over every cell at least once to figure out if its value is $0$ or $1$. Wouldn\'t any $O(mn)$ implementation simply TLE then?

for fourth ques can we solve it such that for each position of one we will check the possibilities surrounding the block whether they are 1 or not in this way we can compute the number of 2x2 grids with 1 , 2 , 3 and 4 ones and then take out the total number of 4X4 grids that can be formed from r rows and c columns and then subtract all other possibilities??\n
I don\'t think fourth (4th) is HARD, I think it is really similar to https://leetcode.com/problems/matrix-block-sum/description/\n
1st one is simple. 2nd one ig gap method. 3rd one using custom comparator sort.
